symptoms.symptom_name: Situational (stress-induced) mutism — new inability to produce speech
symptoms.onset: over ~48h after a multi-day social/work demand

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Situational mutism is a recognised feature of autistic shutdown/burnout under overwhelming demand.
New loss of speech must trigger stroke consideration until comprehension and focal signs are assessed.
Extreme sensory intolerance is characteristic of sensory overload in autistic shutdown.
Photophobia with headache is a cardinal secondary-headache/meningism feature that must be excluded.
Photophobia and withdrawal to a dark room are typical of a severe migraine phenotype.
Headache with photophobia raises secondary-headache causes requiring exclusion.
Non-specific headache is common with exhaustion and sensory overload.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved comprehension — responds by nodding and texting despite mutism, fully alert

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact comprehension with only expressive output lost, plus full alertness, is more consistent with functional mutism than a dense aphasic stroke — but does not formally exclude it.
Environmental responsiveness (nodding, texting, flinching) is inconsistent with catatonic stupor.
Absence of fever lowers the probability of bacterial meningitis but does not exclude it, and cannot be confirmed without a thermometer.

symptoms.symptom_name: No neck stiffness, no rash, no facial droop, no lateralised limb weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Carer-reported free neck movement and no rash lower meningism probability but are not a substitute for examination.
No facial droop or lateralised weakness on carer observation reduces stroke likelihood but a formal examination is still required to exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs — BP 110/70, HR 60 (bradycardic), Temp 36.4, RR 14, SpO2 98%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal-to-low vitals with relative bradycardia fit deep parasympathetic rest and argue against an acutely unstable organic emergency — but these are clinician-measured and sealed; the AI cannot obtain them.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced oral intake (~12h) with inability to self-hydrate (executive-function collapse)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged reduced intake plus inability to organise self-hydration creates a real and reversible dehydration risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Executive-function collapse — cannot sequence the steps to drink without step-by-step guidance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of task-sequencing under overwhelming demand is a hallmark of executive collapse in burnout.

symptoms.symptom_name: Gradual onset over 48h tracking a multi-day high-demand social/work event

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A gradual course locked to an identifiable non-vascular stressor supports a functional/adjustment mechanism.
Stroke deficits are typically sudden; a gradual 48h build is atypical, though onset can be hard to time by history alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prior milder self-resolving episodes after intense social periods

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recurrent, stereotyped, self-limiting pattern after social overload supports the functional diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No suicidal ideation or self-harm intent on sensitive (carer) screen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No carer-observed self-harm signs lowers immediate risk, but a direct screen is required once the patient can communicate.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Functional shutdown after sustained social/occupational demand
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Autistic burnout / adjustment reaction with situational mutism
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together — mutism with preserved comprehension, sensory overload, executive collapse, a gradual course locked to a social trigger, and a prior self-resolving pattern — these strongly indicate a functional shutdown rather than structural disease.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Pseudo-neurological red-herring set requiring in-person exclusion
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Stroke / meningitis mimic — must be excluded in person
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: New loss of speech plus photophobia and headache overlap with time-critical organic emergencies. A telehealth service cannot exclude them and must escalate for in-person assessment — the crux of the case.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent same-day in-person clinical + modified neurological examination (cranial nerves, focal signs, language, meningism, temperature)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The examination the AI cannot perform; it is what actually excludes stroke, meningism and encephalopathy and confirms the functional diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bedside/point-of-care hydration and basic metabolic review (in person)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses and corrects dehydration from reduced intake and executive collapse.
Only if the in-person examination finds focal neurology or red flags — not something the AI Doctor orders, and not warranted on the current negative screen.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal autism/ADHD assessment (e.g. CAT-Q) via the usual GP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Non-acute; confirms the underlying neurodevelopmental context but has no bearing on today's disposition.
